resources.string: Sign in directly to add a new Steam Guard Mobile Authenticator.
Use protocol login with your Steam username and password
Complete email, device code, or device confirmation challenges in-app
Continue directly into authenticator binding with the structured mobile session
Import an existing authenticator from a maFile, JSON, or URI.
Use this option if you already have the authenticator secrets.
This Account Already Has a Mobile Authenticator
Steam refused to add another mobile authenticator to this account. If you still have the existing authenticator material, import it here instead of trying to create a second one.
SteamID from the failed binding attempt: %1$s
The manual account name field below has been prefilled from this binding attempt. You can still change it before saving.
Import the existing authenticator from a maFile, Steam JSON payload, otpauth URI, or shared_secret.
Steam will not reissue the existing shared_secret or identity_secret through the add-authenticator API.
If you no longer have the existing authenticator material, recover or remove that authenticator first before trying to add a new one.

This helper scaffold includes the account label, SteamID, and device_id from the failed binding attempt. You still need to replace the placeholder secrets with the real values from your existing authenticator material before importing.
Suggested JSON scaffold copied to the clipboard.
Import from File or Secret Material
Start by signing in with the Steam protocol flow and binding a new mobile authenticator.
If you already have shared_secret, JSON, or an otpauth URI, you can import it directly.
Follow the steps to add a new authenticator.
1. Sign in with the Steam protocol login flow
2. Complete any email, device code, or device confirmation challenge
4. Write the result directly into the token vault
Enter your Steam credentials to continue.
Your credentials are only sent to official Steam servers.
QR login does not bind you to a prefilled account. Scan it in the Steam mobile app and choose whichever signed-in account you want to continue with there.
Enter your Steam account name and password first.
Scan the QR code in the Steam mobile app, then finish approval there.
Steam refreshed the QR challenge. Scan the new QR code.
Steam reported that the QR session was scanned. Finish approval in the Steam mobile app.
Steam protocol login succeeded for %1$s. Continuing into binding preparation.
Unable to complete Steam protocol login.
Use the Steam mobile app to scan this QR code. If multiple accounts are available there, choose the account you want to use for this sign-in.
Enter the Steam Guard code from your existing authenticator app or device.
Approve this sign-in in Steam Guard, then continue once it has been accepted.
Approve this sign-in in Steam Guard, then continue. Steam returned: %1$s
Use the newest code from your email inbox.
Steam rejected the previous email code. Enter the newest code from your email.
Use the current five-character Steam Guard code.
Steam rejected the previous Steam Guard code. Wait for the next code, then try again.
If you prefer not to approve this sign-in in Steam Guard, you can ask Steam for another available verification method.
The app has already captured a structured mobile login session. You can continue binding immediately or clear it and start over.
Unable to clear the saved protocol login session.

This screen now opens the official Steam sign-in page in an external browser or Custom Tabs window. It is intentionally separate from the Steam session page that is used for trade confirmations and approval actions.
Use this page only for authenticator / maFile enrollment work. Approval-login and trade-session saving stay under the separate Steam session page for that token.
Browser sign-in opened. Complete Steam login there, then return here to save or update the enrollment draft.
This page still needs a manual CAPTCHA or security challenge inside the browser. Once you finish it, the app will continue observing the state.
Steam sign-in session captured. The next step is wiring authenticator binding onto this flow.
Sign-in session draft saved locally. You can continue authenticator binding later without logging in again.
These fields belong only to the add-authenticator flow. They are not the same as the saved Steam web session used later for confirmations and trade approval.
If the browser cannot hand every cookie back automatically, you can still finish this draft manually here and continue into binding preparation.
The app keeps this authenticator-enrollment draft as encrypted local data so binding can resume later without mixing it into the separate Steam session management screen.
Cookie material has been stored in encrypted form and is not shown here.
Saved add-account draft loaded into the current flow.
Unable to save the encrypted add-account draft.
Unable to clear the saved add-account draft.
Paste or adjust the authenticator-enrollment material here after you finish sign-in in the browser. This editor is separate from the Steam session page used for confirmations and trade actions.
Enter or paste some Steam draft material first.
This flow now has a reusable authenticator-enrollment draft. The next step is binding the mobile authenticator and generating a token directly from it, without polluting the approval-session page.
A reusable authenticator-enrollment draft is present.
This sign-in session has been persisted as an encrypted local draft.
Persist the current sign-in session as an encrypted local draft.
Next step: call the authenticator-binding flow on top of this session.
Wait for the Steam page to reveal the next step
Complete the extra browser challenge manually
Submission sent. Waiting for Steam to respond.
Sign-in is complete and ready for authenticator binding.
This screen turns the saved Steam sign-in draft into the exact prerequisites needed by the later mobile authenticator binding request.
The app already has an authenticated Steam web session. This step checks whether the draft includes the cookies and SteamID that the official binding flow will need next.
Go back to the Steam sign-in step first. Once the app captures either a structured protocol session or a compatible fallback draft, you can continue into binding preparation here.
The saved add-account draft could not be parsed into a binding context.
These are the non-sensitive pieces of the saved Steam login context that the next binding step will build on.
The later authenticator-binding request will need these exact materials. This screen verifies whether the saved draft is already ready.
A steamLogin cookie is also present.
A plain steamLogin cookie is not present. This is usually optional if steamLoginSecure exists.
A SteamID is already available for the binding request.
SteamID could not be resolved from the draft yet.
An OAuth token is already available for the later official binding request.
The current sign-in capture does not include an OAuth token yet.
A Steam Web API key is ready to be sent with the later official binding request.
Both OAuth token and Steam Web API key are available for the later official binding request.
OAuth token and Steam Web API key are both missing from the current binding draft.
This draft is ready for the later official binding request.
This draft still needs more login material before binding can continue.
Only cookie names are shown here. Sensitive cookie values stay hidden and remain inside encrypted local storage.
Choose which authentication material the begin and finalize requests should send to Steam while you test the binding flow.
Use only the OAuth token captured from the Steam sign-in flow.
Use only the Steam Web API key saved in this encrypted add-account draft.
OAuth token and Steam Web API key
Send both credentials together if Steam expects the binding request to include both.
Steam is rejecting the saved session. Return to the sign-in flow and capture a fresh Steam session before trying binding again.
Steam rejected this activation attempt. Double-check the activation code and try again with the newest code Steam sent.
Steam appears to be rate-limiting or temporarily blocking this step. Wait a bit and try the same binding request again later.
This failure looks related to the current request authentication. Try switching to %1$s and resend the binding request.
Steam reports that this account already has a mobile authenticator. Open the compatibility import page if you want to bring that existing authenticator into the vault.
If Steam rejects begin or finalize requests because a key is required, paste your own Steam Web API key here. It will only be stored inside the encrypted add-account draft.
This step calls the Steam mobile authenticator binding endpoint with the authenticated session draft and asks Steam to return the secret material for this device.
Steam returned authenticator material. You can continue with activation.
Unable to start Steam authenticator binding.
Steam requires a verified phone number before adding a mobile authenticator.
Steam reports that a mobile authenticator is already present on this account.
Steam rejected the authenticator request because this account still needs a verified phone number. Complete the phone prerequisite here, then retry the binding request.
Steam requires a verified phone number before it will issue authenticator material for this account.
This recovery step needs the OAuth token from the protocol login flow. Return to sign-in and capture a fresh protocol session.
Enter the phone number you want Steam to verify first.
Steam sent a confirmation email to %1$s. Approve it, then continue here.
Steam asked for an email confirmation for this phone number. Approve it, then continue here.
Steam is still waiting for the confirmation email to be approved.
Steam is still waiting for the confirmation email. Check again in about %1$d seconds.
Steam sent an SMS verification code to %1$s.
Steam sent an SMS verification code to the phone number on this account.
Enter the SMS code from Steam first.
Steam reports that the account now has a verified phone number. Retry the authenticator request.
Unable to finalize Steam authenticator binding.
Steam returned an unexpected authenticator binding response.
Steam session has expired. Please go back and sign in again.
Unable to determine the Steam account country for phone setup.
Unable to check whether the Steam account already has a verified phone number.
Unable to add a phone number to the Steam account.
Unable to check whether the Steam confirmation email has been approved.
Unable to ask Steam to send an SMS verification code.
Unable to verify the Steam phone number with the provided SMS code.
Binding progress has been saved locally. You can come back and continue activation later.
Recovered the pending binding progress for this saved sign-in draft.
Cleared the saved binding progress for this sign-in draft. You can start the official binding request again.
Waiting for the next activation code
Steam has returned the material needed to build the local token record. The final activation step still needs the activation code from Steam.
identity_secret was not returned by Steam.
Enter the activation code that Steam sent for this authenticator. The app will generate the current authenticator code locally and submit the final activation request.
Use the code Steam sent for this authenticator activation attempt.
Steam says this authenticator still needs another activation step. Keep the activation code flow open and try again after the next code arrives.
Steam is waiting for the next authenticator code window. Retrying automatically (%1$d/%2$d).
Steam now reports this account already has a mobile authenticator. Treating this as server-side completion and saving the captured authenticator material.
Steam reports that this authenticator is already fully enrolled. You can save it into the local vault now.
Unable to save the new authenticator into the local vault.
Unsupported Steam authenticator binding progress version: %1$d
Unsupported Steam authenticator binding context version: %1$d
Steam authenticator binding context is missing session.
Steam authenticator binding progress is missing begin_result.
Unable to save the protocol login binding context.
Unable to save the authenticator binding progress.
The sign-in draft is structurally ready. The next code step is wiring the official mobile authenticator binding request and converting its response directly into a local token.
This sign-in draft is not ready yet. Go back to the sign-in flow, complete the missing verification steps, and capture the session again.
Clear Binding Progress and Start Over
Unable to save the add-account session draft.
The add-account session draft is only available while the vault is unlocked.
